steps.title: Spot the defining contrast about bumblebees
steps.content: The passage presents bumblebees both as the primary scientific model for understanding wild-bee declines and as possessing unique resilience traits (social behavior and dietary generalism) that distinguish them from other wild-bee genera.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to reach Ghisbain's conclusion
steps.content:
Ghisbain concludes that while bumblebees serve as a useful research model, their special traits mean their responses to environmental threats may not match those of wild bees lacking similar resilience factors.

This warrants caution when extrapolating from bumblebees to other species.

The other choices either predict bumblebee stability (contradicted by their documented collapse), reject the modeling practice the passage validates, or focus on unrelated threat comparisons.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
